course_title: Foundational Social Work Field Practice Course in Health and Ageing

learning_outcomes.description: Describe the key issues, challenges, core principles, and frameworks within a range of health services

Demonstrate a critical awareness of the impact of the social determinants of health, health transitions, trauma, acute care, and elder abuse and consider the implications for practice with individuals and families.
Demonstrate a foundational understanding of the ethical considerations and relevant legislative frameworks in health service practice including for people with impaired capacity.
Demonstrate competence at a foundational level in psychosocial assessment and intervention skills with individuals and families across a range of practice settings.

assessments.task_description:
This description is a summary of the task. For full details, including the marking rubric, please refer to the course Learn UQ (Blackboard) site.

Psychosocial assessments are core social work practice and a reflection of competence in relationship and process skills as well as analytic and reasoning skills. A psychosocial assessment is a key mechanism for developing an analysis of the ‘person-in-context’, an appropriate intervention plan, and is effective for communicating the social work role to other health care professionals.

This task involves observing a social work interview during the tutorial in Week 9. The task is to independently complete a written psychosocial assessment based on your observation of the interview. The task will be completed independently before the due date.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assessment requires students to submit an electronic version of their assignment via Blackboard. The electronic version of your assignment will be submitted via Blackboard, using the Turnitin software.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
End of Semester Exam Period

7/11/2026 - 21/11/2026

The end of semester exam will be held on campus. The exam is a closed book exam.
assessments.task_description: The end-of-semester exam will cover all course materials, including lectures, tutorials, and required readings. It will comprise a number of case studies, with each case study followed by questions designed to assess students’ understanding and application of course concepts.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Social Determinants of Health (Wk 1)

Welcome to this course. The week's lecture covers:

  1. Social Determinants of Health. Inequities in access to health care.
  2. Life Course perspective.
  3. Vulnerable groups.

A separate video will set out an overview of the course, expectations, modes of delivery, and assessment.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Social Determinants of Health (Wk 1) Welcome to this course. The week's lecture covers: Social Determinants of Health. Inequities in access to health care. Life Course perspective. Vulnerable groups. A separate video will set out an overview of the course, expectations, modes of delivery, and assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Social Determinants of Health (Wk 1)

In this tutorial we will:

  1. With case examples identify the social determinants of health with links to the social work role in health..
  2. View a video and in groups consider key concepts in life course theory including key events, trajectories, and linked lives.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Social Determinants of Health  (Wk 1) In this tutorial we will: With case examples identify the social determinants of health with links to the social work role in health.. View a video and in groups consider key concepts in life course theory including key events, trajectories, and linked lives.

learning_activities.topic:
Social Work Role in Health (Wk 2)

This week's lecture includes:

  1. Core areas of expertise
  2. Theories used in practice
  3. Skills in practice

We will also contextualise practice with a consideration of the Australia's health care system. We will consider strengths and challenges within Australia's health care.

Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_description: Social Work Role in Health (Wk 2) This week's lecture includes: Core areas of expertise Theories used in practice Skills in practice We will also contextualise practice with a consideration of the Australia's health care system. We will consider strengths and challenges within Australia's health care.

learning_activities.topic:
Social Work Role in Health (Wk 2)

Following on the lecture we will consider practice with older people in the community - we will watch a film and then in groups discuss the practice issues and social work role.

Following on the lecture's consideration of theories informing social work in health we will work through a number of case studies and consider relevant theories.

We will work through a group exercise in relation to evidence based practice following a natural disaster.

Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_description: Social Work Role in Health (Wk 2) Following on the lecture we will consider practice with older people in the community - we will watch a film and then in groups discuss the practice issues and social work role. Following on the lecture's consideration of theories informing social work in health we will work through a number of case studies and consider relevant theories. We will work through a group exercise in relation to evidence based practice following a natural disaster.

learning_activities.topic:
Knowledge & Skills - Grief & Loss (Wk 3)

This week's lecture focuses on working with grief & loss in health settings.

Learning outcomes: L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Knowledge & Skills - Grief & Loss (Wk 3) This week's lecture focuses on working with grief & loss in health settings.

Wednesday is a public holiday; there is no in person tutorial. A number of videos, case studies relating to grief and loss, will be available for you to work through in your own time.

Learning outcomes: L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Knowledge & Skills - Grief & Loss (Wk 3) Wednesday is a public holiday; there is no in person tutorial.  A number of videos, case studies relating to grief and loss, will be available for you to work through in your own time.

learning_activities.topic:
Knowledge & Skills-Legal Ethical Issues (Wk 4)

This week's lecture considers a range of medico-legal issues that are part of the social work role in health care.

These include:

  1. Consent (and self determination)
  2. Confidentiality and Privacy
  3. Capacity and decision-making.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Knowledge & Skills-Legal Ethical Issues (Wk 4) This week's lecture considers a range of medico-legal issues that are part of the social work role in health care. These include: Consent (and self determination) Confidentiality and Privacy Capacity and decision-making.

learning_activities.topic:
Knowledge & Skills-Legal Ethical Issues (Wk 4)

The case studies in this tutorial relate to:

  1. Consent
  2. Privacy and confidentiality
  3. Impaired decision making
  4. Guardianship and Administration

Learning outcomes: L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Knowledge & Skills-Legal Ethical Issues (Wk 4) The case studies in this tutorial relate to: Consent Privacy and confidentiality Impaired decision making Guardianship and Administration

In this lecture we will be discuss a range of legal documents including EPOA and AHD. The role of QCAT will also be discussed.

learning_activities.topic:
Knowledge & Skills - Transitions (Wk 6)

This week's lecture considers transitions. In particular we consider transitions in individual's (and family's) lives linked to diagnosis of a health condition, adjustment to frailty, and disability.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Knowledge & Skills - Transitions (Wk 6) This week's lecture considers transitions. In particular we consider transitions in individual's (and family's) lives linked to diagnosis of a health condition, adjustment to frailty, and disability.

learning_activities.topic:
Knowledge & Skills - Transitions (Wk 6)

We will aim to understand using case studies (documentaries) the nature of transitions and social work responses with:

  1. Dementia
  2. Brain Injury

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04

This week's lecture introduces student's to the components of a structured template used by social workers to document psychosocial assessments in the health record.
